lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, Ross is entering.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Guys? There's a somebody I'd like you to meet.
A monkey jumps on to his shoulder.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oooh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: W-wait. What is that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 'That' would be Marcel. You wanna say hi?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, no, I don't.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, he is precious! Where did you get him?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My friend Bethel rescued him from some lab.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is so cruel! Why? Why would a parent name their child Bethel?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, that monkey's got a Ross on its ass!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, is he gonna live with you, like, in your apartment?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. I mean, it's been kinda quiet since Carol left, so...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why don't you just get a roommate?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nah, I dunno... I think you reach a certain age, having a roommate is kinda pathe- (Realises) ....sorry, that's, that's 'pathet', which is Sanskrit for 'really cool way to live'.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Phoebe is getting ready to sing. Joey is not there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So you guys, I'm doing all new material tonight. I have twelve new songs about my mother's suicide, and one about a snowman.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Might wanna open with the snowman.
Enter Joey

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey, Joey. Hey, buddy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, how'd it go?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahhhhhh, I didn't get the job.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How could you not get it? You were Santa last year.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I dunno. Some fat guy's sleeping with the store manager. He's not even jolly, it's all political.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So what are you gonna be?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, I'm gonna be one of his helpers. It's just such a slap in the face, y'know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, do you guys know what you're doing for New Year's? (They all protest and hit her with cushions) Gee, what?! What is wrong with New Year's?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nothing for you, you have Paolo. You don't have to face the horrible pressures of this holiday: desperate scramble to find anything with lips just so you can have someone to kiss when the ball drops!! Man, I'm talking loud!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, for your information, Paolo is gonna be in Rome this New Year, so I'll be just as pathetic as the rest of you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, you wish!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's just that I'm sick of being a victim of this Dick Clark holiday. I say this year, no dates, we make a pact. Just the six of us- dinner.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah, okay. Alright.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I was hoping for a little more enthusiasm.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Woooo! Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, you're on.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, oh, good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Into microphone) Okay, hi. Ladies and gentlemen, back by popular demand, Miss Phoebe Buffay. Wooh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Takes mike) Thanks, hi. Um, I wanna start with a song that means a lot to me this time of year. (Shakes bell as an introduction) (Sung:)
I made a man with eyes of coal
And a smile so bewitchin',
How was I supposed to know
That my mom was dead in the kitchen?
shakes bell) La lalala la la la la lalala la la...
Cut to later. Everyone is totally depressed by now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Sung)
...My mother's ashes
Even her eyelashes
Are resting in a little yellow jar,
And sometimes when it's breezy...
Over the sound of Phoebe singing we hear two scientists, Max and David, having a noisy discussion

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Sung)
...I feel a little sneezy
And now I- (abruptly stops
Excuse me, excuse me! Yeah, noisy boys! (They stop talking and look up) Is it something that you would like to share with the entire group?

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: No. No, that's- that's okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, c'mon, if it's important enough to discuss while I'm playing, then I assume it's important enough for everyone else to hear!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Quietly, to the others) That guy's going home with a note!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Noth- I was- I was just saying to my-

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Could you speak up please?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: (Stands up and speaks more loudly) Sorry, I wa- I was just saying to my friend that I thought you were the most beautiful woman that I'd ever seen in my- in my life. And then he said that- you said you thought

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: Daryl Hannah.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Daryl Hannah was the most beautiful woman that he'd ever seen in his life and I said yeah, I liked her in Splash, a lot, but not so much in- in Wall Street, I thought she had kind of a

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: Hard quality.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: -hard quality. And uh, while Daryl Hannah is beautiful in a conventional way, you are luminous with a kind of a delicate grace. Then, uh, that-that-that's when you started yelling. (Sits down)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, we're gonna take a short break. (Goes over to their table)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, that guy's going home with more than a note!
Monica and Rachel's, everyone except Joey is decorating for Christmas.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come here, Marcel. Sit here. (Marcel wanders off)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs, I can't believe he hasn't kissed you yet. I mean God, by my sixth date with Paolo, I mean he had already named both my breasts! ...Ooh. Did I just share too much?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just a smidge.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: David's like, y'know, Scientist Guy. He's very methodical.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think it's romantic.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Me too! Oh! Did you ever see An Officer and a Gentleman?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, he's kinda like the guy I went to see that with. Except, except he-he's smarter, and gentler, and sweeter... I just- I just wanna be with him all the time. Day and night, and night and day... and special occasions...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait a minute, wait a minute, I see where this is going, you're gonna ask him to New Year's, aren't you. You're gonna break the pact. She's gonna break the pact.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, no, no, no, no. Yeah, could I just?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, 'cause I already asked Janice.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: C'mon, this was a pact! This was your pact!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I snapped, okay? I couldn't handle the pressure and I snapped.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, but Janice? That-that was like the worst breakup in history!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not saying it was a good idea, I'm saying I snapped!
[Joey enters, his shoes have bells on, which jingle as he walks. He is wearing a long coat.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi. Hi, sorry I'm late.
He removes the coat to reveal an elf costume

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Too many jokes... must mock Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nice shoes, huh? (He wiggles his foot and the bells tinkle)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Aah, y'killing me!
Marcel knocks over some kitchen tools

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross! He's playing with my spatulas again!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, look, he's not gonna hurt them, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you always have to bring him here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I didn't wanna leave him alone. Alright? We- we had our first fight this morning. I think it has to do with my working late. I said some things that I didn't mean, and he- he threw some faeces...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, if you're gonna work late, I could look in on him for you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, that'd be great! Okay, but if you do, make sure it seems like you're there to see him, okay, and you're not like doing it as a favour to me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, but if he asks, I'm not going to lie.
Max and David's lab, David is explaining something to Phoebe with the aid of a whiteboard.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: ...But, you can't actually test this theory, because today's particle accelerators are nowhere near powerful enough to simulate these conditions.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, alright, I have a question, then.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yuh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, were you planning on kissing me ever?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Uh, that's definitely a, uh, valid question. And, uh, the answer would be (Writes YES on the board) yes. Yes I was. But, see, I wanted it to be this phenomenal kiss that happened at this phenomenal moment, because, well, 'cause it's you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Right. But, see, the longer I waited, the more phenomenal the kiss had to be, and now we've reached a place where it's just gotta be one of those things where I just like... sweep everything off the table and throw you down on it. And, uh, I'm not really a, uh, sweeping sorta fella.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, David, I, I think you are a sweeping sorta fella. I mean, you're a sweeper! ...trapped inside a physicist's body.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Rrrreally.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah, oh, I'm sure of it. You should just do it, just sweep and throw me.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: ...Now? Now?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, right now.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Okay, okay, okay. (Gets ready to sweep, and then picks up a laptop computer) Y'know what, this was just really expensive. (Puts it down elsewhere. Then picks up a microscope) And I'll take- this was a gift. (Moves it)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, now you're just kinda tidying.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Okay, what the hell, what the hell. (Sweeps the remaining papers off the desk and grabs Phoebe) You want me to actually throw you or you-you wanna just hop?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can hop. (She hops onto the table)
They kiss, finally
Central Perk, everyone is there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So tell me something. What does the phrase 'no date pact' mean to you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry, okay. It's just that Chandler has somebody, and Phoebe has somebody- I thought I'd ask Fun Bobby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fun Bobby? Your ex-boyfriend Fun Bobby?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You know more than one Fun Bobby?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I happen to know a Fun Bob.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Brings Joey a mug of coffee) Okay, here we go...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh ooh ooh ooh, there's no room for milk!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Glances at Joey and then sips his coffee) There. Now there is.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, so on our no-date evening, three of you now have dates.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, four.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Four.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Five.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Five. (Buries his head in his hands)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sorry. Paolo's catching an earlier flight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, and I met this really hot single mom at the store. What's an elf to do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, so I'm gonna be the only one standing there alone when the ball drops?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, c'mon. We'll have, we'll have a big party, and no-one'll know who's with who.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y'know, this is so not what I needed right now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, it's-it's Marcel. He keeps shutting me out, y'know? He's walking around all the time dragging his hands...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's so weird, I had such a blast with him the other night.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, we played, we watched TV.. that juggling thing is amazing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, uh... what juggling thing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: With the balled-up socks? I figured you taught him that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, it wasn't that big a deal. He just balled up socks... and a melon...
Max runs in

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: Phoebe. Hi.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, hi Max! Hey, do you know everybody?

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: No. Have you seen David?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, he hasn't been around.

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: Well, if you see him, tell him to pack his bags. We are going to Minsk.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Minsk?

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: Minsk. It's in Russia.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know where Minsk is.

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: We got the grant. Three years, all expenses paid.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So when, when do you leave?

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: January first.
Commercial Break
Max and David's lab, they are working. Phoebe knocks on the door

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Hi! (Kisses her) What-what're you doing here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, well, Max told me about Minsk, so (Puts on a fake cheery voice) congratulations! This is so exciting!

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: It'd be even more exciting if we were going.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you're not going? (Fake disappointed voice) Oh, why?

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: Tell her, David. 'I don't wanna go to Minsk and work with Lifson and Yamaguchi and Flench, on nonononononono. I wanna stay here and make out with my girlfriend!!' (Storms out)

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Thank you, Max. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So-so you're really not going?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I don't know. I don't know what I'm gonna do. I just- you decide.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh don't do that.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Please.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no no.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: No, but I'm asking-

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, but I can't do that-

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: No, but I can't-

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's your thing, and-

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: -make the decision-

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, um, stay.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Stay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Stay.
He thinks for a moment and sweeps the stuff off the table

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Getting so good at that! (She hops on)

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: It was Max's stuff. (They kiss)
Monica and Rachel's, the party has started.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I love this artichoke thing! Oh, don't tell me what's in it, the diet starts tomorrow! (Laughs her Janice laugh)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You remember Janice.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Vividly.
Someone knocks on the door; Monica gets it

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Hi, I'm Sandy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sandy! Hi! C'mon in! (She enters, followed by a young boy and a younger girl)...You brought your kids.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Yeah. That's okay, right?
Joey and Monica look at each other and shrug. Ross enters with Marcel on his shoulder

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Par-tay!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That thing is not coming in here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 'That thing'? This is how you greet guests at a party? Let me ask you something, if I showed up here with my new girlfriend, she wouldn't be welcome in your home?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm guessing your new girlfriend wouldn't urinate on my coffee table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. He was more embarrassed about that than anyone. Okay? And for him to have the courage to walk back in here like nothing happened...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright. Just keep him away from me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you. (She walks off) C'mon, Marcel, whaddya say you and I do a little mingling? (Marcel runs off) Alright, I'll, uh... catch up with you later.
The door opens. Rachel is standing there. Her coat is muddy and torn, her hair is dishevelled and her face is bruised. Everyone turns to look

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my gosh! Rachel, honey.. are you okay? Where-where's Paolo?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Rome. Jerk missed his flight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And then... your face is bloated?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. Okay. I was at the airport, getting into a cab, when this woman- this blonde planet with a pocketbook- starts yelling at me. Something about how it was her cab first. And then the next thing I know she just starts- starts pulling me out by my hair! So I'm blowing my attack whistle thingy and three more cabs show up, and as I'm going to get into a cab she tackles me. And I hit my head on the kerb and cut my lip on my whistle...oh...everybody having fun at the party? (To Monica) Are people eating my dip?
[Time lapse. Monica and Rachel, fixed up somewhat, emerge from a bedroom]

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Y'know, when I saw you at the store last week, it was probably the first time I ever mentally undressed an elf.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow, that's, uh, dirty.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Yeah.
They almost kiss and then Joey realises her kids are staring at them

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, kids...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Watching Marcel play with Phoebe. To Chandler) Look at him. I'm not saying he has to spend the whole evening with me, but at least check in.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: (Startles them) There you are! Haaah, you got away from me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Imitating) But you found me!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Here, Ross, take our picture. (Hands him a camera and he starts snapping) Smile! You're on Janice Camera!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Kill me. Kill me now.
Someone else knocks on the door. Monica looks through the spyhole

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey everybody! It's Fun Bobby!
Everyone cheers. Monica opens the door. Bobby is obviously very depressed

lines.speaker: Fun Bobby
lines.text: Hey, sorry I'm late. But my, uh, grandfather, he- died about two hours ago. But I-I-I couldn't get a flight out 'til tomorrow, so here I am!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Approaching) Hey Fun Bobby! Whoah! Who died?
Monica gestures wildly behind Fun Bobby's back
[Time lapse. Bobby is talking about his grandfather. Everyone else is virtually in tears]

lines.speaker: Fun Bobby
lines.text: It's gonna be an open casket, y'know, so at least I'll- I get to see him again.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: (Ross is still taking their photo) Oh, I'm gonna blow this one up, and I'm gonna write 'Reunited' in glitter.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright, Janice, that's it! Janice... Janice... Hey, Janice, when I invited you to this party I didn't necessarily think that it meant that we-

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, no. Oh, no.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry you misunderstood...

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh my God. You listen to me, Chandler, you listen to me. One of these times is just gonna be your last chance with me. (She runs off)
Ross is still taking photos

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, will you give me the thing. (Snatches the camera)
David is feeding Phoebe popcorn. Max walks up

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi, Max!

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: Yoko. (To David) I've decided to go to Minsk without you.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: Max
lines.text: It won't be the same- but it'll still be Minsk. Happy New Year.(Walks off)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you alright?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah, I'm fine, I'm fine.
Phoebe leads David into a bedroom

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're going to Minsk.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: No, I'm... not going to Minsk.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you are so going to Minsk. You belong in Minsk. You can't stay here just 'cause of me.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yes I can. Because if I go it means I have to break up with you, and I can't break up with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yes, yes, yes you can. Just say, um, 'Phoebe, my work is my life and that's what I have to do right now'. And I say 'your work?! Your work?! How can you say that?!'. And then you say, um, 'it's tearing me apart, but I have no choice. Can't you understand that?'. And I say (Hits him) 'no! No! I can't understand that!'.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Uh, ow.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, sorry. Um, and, and then you put your arms around me. And then you put your arms around me. (He does so) And, um, and then you tell me that you love me and you'll never forget me.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I'll never forget you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And then you say that it's almost midnight and you have to go because you don't wanna start the new year with me if you can't finish it. (They kiss) I'm gonna miss you. You scientist guy.

lines.speaker: Dick Clark
lines.text: (on TV) Hi, this is Dick Clark, live in Times Square. We're in a virtual snowstorm of confetti here in Times Square...
Joey puts a blanket over Sandy's kids

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There y'go, kids.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To a woman who he has clearly just met) And then the peacock bit me. (Laughs) Please kiss me at midnight. (She leaves)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You seen Sandy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh. Uh, I don't know how to tell you this, but she's in Monica's bedroom, getting it on with Max, that scientist geek. Ooh, look at that, I did know how to tell you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Vrrbddy, the bll is drrbing.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: (in the kitchen) What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: The bll is drrbing!

lines.speaker: Dick Clark
lines.text: (on TV) In twenty seconds it'll be midnight...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And the moment of joy is upon us.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Looks like that no date pact thing worked out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Everybody looks so happy. I hate that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Not everybody's happy. Hey Bobby!
Bobby waves and then bursts into tears. Midnight comes and everyone at the party except for the gang cheers and kisses

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I uh.. just thought I'd throw this out here. I'm no math whiz, but I do believe there are three girls and three guys right here. (Makes kiss noise)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I dunno. I don't feel like kissing anyone tonight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't kiss anyone.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So I'm kissing everyone?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nonono, you can't kiss Ross, that's your brother.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Perfect. Perfect. So now everybody's getting kissed but me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright, somebody kiss me. Somebody kiss me, it's midnight! Somebody kiss me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alrightalrightalright. (Kisses him. Ross takes a photo) There.
Closing Credits
Monica and Rachel's, time lapse.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Watching Marcel and talking to Rachel) I wanted this to work so much. I mean I'm still in there, changing his diapers, pickin' his fleas... but he's just phoning it in. Just so hard to accept the fact that something you love so much doesn't love you back.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ...I think that bitch cracked my tooth.
End
